name: v1.0.244
body:
## ✨ New: Ollama connection lives in the chat header

Point cockpit at any Ollama endpoint without touching env vars. Open the **Ollama model picker** in the chat header and you'll find new **Server URL** and **API Key** sections — mirroring the DeepSeek flow.

- `baseUrl` and `apiKey` are saved together in `~/.cockpit/ollama/config.json`, kept out of `settings.json` so the key never ships to the browser
- Resolution order per field: **config file > env > default** — the picker shows the effective value (with a masked key) and a hint about where it came from
- Changing the server URL refreshes the model list in place; `start` no longer hardcodes `localhost:11434`

> Running Ollama on another host or behind an auth proxy? Paste the URL + key into the picker and go.

## ✨ New: Ollama transcripts get cleaned up

Ollama chat sessions in `~/.cockpit/ollama-sessions/` used to accumulate forever. A daily retention sweep now ages out whole sessions past **`COCKPIT_SESSION_KEEP_DAYS`** (default 30), mirroring Claude Code's cleanup. Pinned sessions are always protected, and the sweep only ever touches `ollama-sessions/` — other engines manage their own history.

## 🐛 Fix: per-tool-call snapshots no longer silently dropped

The shadow-git snapshot taken on each mutating tool call could fatal its whole `git add` batch on a single edge-case path (a deletion, an ignored-but-tracked file, a path that vanished mid-scan) — and some cases poisoned the shadow index so the failure kept recurring. Each rejection class is now handled individually, so snapshots survive these paths instead of going missing.

## 🐛 Fix: readable code blocks in dark-mode notes

Code blocks in the note editor were nearly invisible in dark mode — their background sat one lightness step off the card surface. Inline and fenced code now use a distinct shade with visible contrast in both themes.

## 🐛 Fix: chat tabs show more of the title

Chat tab titles were truncated at 128px even with plenty of horizontal room. The cap is now 260px, so titles show more fully; min width and overflow-scroll behavior are unchanged.

name: v1.0.243
body: ## ✨ New: image previews in the tool-call list Read / Edit / Write calls on image files (`.png`, `.jpg`, …) now show up in the same clickable list as `.md` and `.html`, and open in the fullscreen preview. Images are streamed as raw bytes (``), so opening a large screenshot never pulls its contents into the JS heap. ## ✨ New: session titles in recent-sessions cards The recent-sessions sidebar and its hover popover now show the session's AI-generated title beneath the project name, matching the project/session list. The title is re-derived from disk (aiTitle > summary > first message) on every snapshot, so a title that lands *after* a run finishes shows up immediately instead of being stuck on the first user message. Old stale cards self-heal with no migration. ## 🐛 Fix: session switching no longer freezes the UI Switching chat sessions could freeze the app for several seconds on a real `~/.cockpit` — a single unstable prop was defeating the `React.memo` on each always-mounted heavy renderer, so every switch re-parsed and re-highlighted the whole chat, the previewed markdown doc, and the console bubbles. Chat, Explorer, and Console renderers now hold their memo across a switch, and the server-side global-state snapshot caches parsed transcripts by fingerprint. ## 🐛 Fix: DeepSeek API key no longer ships to the browser The DeepSeek API key lived in `settings.json`, which `GET /api/settings` returns to the browser. It now lives in a dedicated `~/.cockpit/deepseek/credentials.json`; the API only ever exposes `{ hasKey, maskedKey }`, never the raw key. Existing keys already in `settings.json` are not migrated automatically — re-enter the key once to move it into the credential file. ## 🐛 Fix: admin locked out of a review they just closed Closing a review deactivated it, and the next poll returned "review not found" to the admin who closed it — admin-ness was computed client-side, so the server couldn't tell a local owner from a revoked LAN viewer. The server now stamps an unforgeable local-peer header at boot and exempts the local admin from the closed-review 404, while remote/LAN revocation stays intact. ## 🐛 Fix: selection toolbar in the tool-call diff viewer Selecting text in the diff viewer opened from a message's FileDiff icon never surfaced the comment / send-to-AI / search toolbar — comments weren't enabled on that `DiffView`, and its floating toolbar had no container to portal into. Both gates are fixed, so the toolbar now appears there like it does everywhere else. ## 🐛 Fix: "Send to AI" button contrast The "Send to AI" button used a subtle-background text color on its solid teal fill, leaving too little contrast in both themes. It now uses white text.

name: v1.0.242
body:
## 🐛 Fix: clicking a session opens *that* session

Selecting a session for a project that wasn't open yet could land you in a
**different** chat than the one you clicked. The picked session id was handed
to the freshly-loaded panel through a one-shot message that raced the panel's
own session loading — and usually lost, defaulting you to the most recent
session instead.

The selected session is now frozen at project open and carried in the panel
URL, so it activates deterministically. This covers every entry point that
shares the mechanism: the Session Browser, the empty-state picker, desktop
deep links, chat fork / project-sessions modal, recent & pinned sessions,
scheduled tasks, and session-complete toasts.

## 🐛 Fix: snapshots keep recording across symlinked dirs

If a previously-tracked file's parent directory became a symlink (e.g.
`.claude/skills/explain` linked out to `.agents/skills/explain`), git reported
the old path as a deletion and the whole snapshot batch aborted with
`beyond a symbolic link` — so that project silently stopped getting tool-call
snapshots, permanently.

Deletions are now staged index-only (`git rm --cached`) instead of through
`git add`, which sidesteps the check. Affected shadow repos self-heal on their
next snapshot; no action needed.

## 📚 Docs: Notes editor keymap

The Notes docs now list the org-mode keyboard shortcuts — `Tab` folding,
`Alt`/`Shift`+arrow to move and re-nest items — and document the
todo / doing / done tri-state on task lists.

name: v1.0.241
body:
## ✨ New: `/ap` apply-mode slash command

A new builtin sits alongside `/qa`: type **`/ap`** to have the agent implement a `<SPEC>` while keeping a running **`apply-notes.html`** of every out-of-spec decision, change, and tradeoff it made along the way. When the spec is silent or you have to deviate, the reasoning lands in the notes file instead of getting lost in the transcript — so you can review what the agent chose, not just what it wrote.

```text
/ap implement the retry backoff described in RFC-12
```

`/ap` is available in the slash-command autocomplete (bilingual prompt, ZH/EN).

## ✨ New: org-mode keyboard shortcuts in the note editor

The Note editor gains an org-mode-style keyboard layer for restructuring a document without touching the mouse:

- **Tab** — cycle heading fold (expanded → folded → children), cascading to all descendants. The gutter arrow still toggles a single heading.
- **Alt+↑ / Alt+↓** — move a line up/down. A heading carries its whole subtree; paragraph/todo/list lines hop across types and list boundaries within a section (headings, code, tables, and quotes act as hard walls).
- **Alt+← / Alt+→** — cycle a line's rank on the ring H1 → H2 → H3 → paragraph; list items outdent/indent.
- **Shift+← / Shift+→** — cycle todo state on the ring paragraph → todo → doing → done, converting between a plain line and a task item at the boundary.

Pasting notes into other apps is cleaner too: task items emit leaner clipboard HTML and a single-newline plain-text serializer, so the line count survives the round-trip.

## 🐛 Fix: keyboard focus rings

Two related focus-ring glitches are gone:

- **Double ring on inputs.** A global `:focus-visible` rule was overriding Tailwind's `focus:outline-none`, stacking the themed ring on top of a component's own `focus:ring-*` — most visibly a thick double ring on the chat textarea. The rule now lives in `@layer base`, so inputs and editors keep their own ring while plain buttons still get the themed keyboard focus ring.
- **Stuck ring after ESC.** Closing an overlay with ESC left its trigger button showing a mismatched blue focus ring, because the handler flipped state without blurring the trigger. A shared `useEscToClose` hook now blurs on close across the A-group modals (DiffViewer, TokenStats, TodoViewer, UserMessages, AskQuestionViewer, CommitDetail, SkillPreview).

## 🐛 Fix: jump-to-search in Code Map mode

Jumping to a search match while the Explorer is in Code Map mode now leaves Code Map first, so the CodeViewer remounts and actually scrolls to the target line.

name: v1.0.240
body:
## ✨ New: File diffs open in the Explorer, not a modal

Click the **file-diff icon** on an assistant message and the changes now open **inside the Explorer panel** (panel 2) with an auto-swipe, instead of a full-screen overlay. You stay in the three-panel flow — the diff sits above the file browser, re-firing on another message swaps the content in place, and closing leaves you on Explorer where you can keep browsing.

The subagent transcript diff (which has no second panel to swipe to) still uses the familiar modal.

## ✨ New: Steadier session & tab titles

Tab titles now prefer the session's stable `aiTitle` over the Claude Code CLI `summary` line. The old summary drifted as it accumulated across compactions, so long sessions would show a stale title; `aiTitle` is a single stable value per session.

New title priority: **manual rename → aiTitle → summary → first user message → "Untitled Session"**.

name: v1.0.239
body:
## ✨ New: per-tool-call project snapshots

Cockpit now snapshots your project after **every mutating AI tool call**, across all engines (Claude / Codex / Kimi / DeepSeek / Ollama), and lets you browse the changes one tool call at a time — like git history, but scoped to exactly what the agent just did.

A shadow git repo per project (`~/.cockpit/snapshots/…`) records one commit per mutating call; **your project's own `.git` is never touched** and `.gitignore` is honored. Click the **FileDiff icon** on a message to open the change viewer: a per-call timeline on the left, a real on-disk diff on the right. Bash/Task calls read as `[Bash] Install project dependencies` instead of a bare `[Bash]`, and calls that only touch tests or docs get a subdued **test/docs** chip so the critical edits stand out.

Retention is automatic (7-day branches, 30-day repo TTL) with runaway guards (2 MB/file, 50k-file / 1 GB skip). Three env vars tune it:

```bash
COCKPIT_SNAPSHOT_KEEP_DAYS      # per-day branch retention (default 7)
COCKPIT_SNAPSHOT_REPO_TTL_DAYS  # whole-repo TTL (default 30)
COCKPIT_SNAPSHOT_MAX_FILE_KB    # per-file size cap
```

New docs page (**Agent → Snapshots**) and a blog post, *"Review What Your AI Actually Changed — One Tool Call at a Time,"* walk through it.

## ✨ New: a better diff viewer

The change viewer gained a **split / unified** toggle (双栏/单栏, defaults to split) and a **compact / full** density toggle (精简/全文) that collapses unchanged runs. The FileDiff icon is now gated at render time, so a message with zero real changes never shows a dead button, and the viewer keeps your selected commit, file, and scroll position while the AI keeps editing.

## ✨ New: open local HTML in a browser bubble

Type a path ending in `.html` / `.htm` in the Console input and it opens in a **browser bubble** instead of running as a shell command — mirroring the existing `*.ipynb → notebook bubble` behavior.

## ✨ New: send code straight to the AI from comments

Comment cards and the Comments modal gained **send-to-AI** entries, and the HTML preview now has a selection toolbar — so you can push a highlighted snippet or an annotation into chat without retyping it.

## 🐛 Fix: search & comment polish

- Reopening **Cmd+F** search now keeps your last keyword and adds an inline clear button.
- In AI messages, the blank line between the last quoted reference and your question is gone — the question sits directly under the reference.

name: v1.0.238
body:
## ✨ New: in-progress state for note todos

Todos in the **Note editor** now have a third state. Click a todo's marker to cycle **todo → doing → done**:

- `[ ]` todo — empty marker
- `[/]` doing — orange marker, the same color as the running-task indicator in the tab bar
- `[x]` done — teal check

Notes stay plain Markdown (`- [/] my task`), so files remain readable in any external editor, and existing `[ ]` / `[x]` items keep working byte-for-byte unchanged. Screen readers get the three states as `aria-checked` false / mixed / true.

Everything else in this release is internal cleanup (lint config, comment-language policy) — no other user-visible changes.

name: v1.0.237
body:
## 🐛 Fix: chat breaking after `cockpit update`

Running `cockpit update` could leave the Claude chat engine broken with a cryptic `Native CLI binary for darwin-arm64 not found` error — surfacing only once you sent a message. The cause: an in-place `npm i -g` upgrade can skip the Claude Agent SDK's platform-specific native binary (an optional dependency) when the SDK version bumps.

`cockpit update` now installs with that optional binary included and self-repairs (a clean reinstall) if it's still missing, so chat keeps working across upgrades. Cockpit also checks for the binary at install time and on startup, printing a clear one-line fix instead of failing silently mid-chat.

If you already hit this, run `cockpit update` once more, or reinstall:

```bash
npm uninstall -g @surething/cockpit && npm install -g @surething/cockpit
```

name: v1.0.236
body:
## ✨ New: PDF preview in the file browser

Open a `.pdf` straight from the Explorer and read it inline. The viewer follows your light/dark theme, only renders the pages currently in view (so scrolling stays smooth on large documents), and gives you a thumbnail rail on the left for click-to-jump navigation with scroll-synced highlighting.

JSON files get the same unified treatment now: a single **Preview / Exit** toggle (on by default) renders `.json` in a readable, theme-aware layout, instead of the old separate "readable" switch.

## ✨ New: Notes — outline folding + conflict-safe saves

Two upgrades to the note editor:

- **Collapsible headings.** Click the arrow beside any heading to fold everything under it, down to the next same-or-higher heading. The fold state is saved with the note (as an invisible `<!-- fold -->` marker), so it survives reopening — and stays invisible in external Markdown editors.
- **No more silently lost edits.** If the same note changed elsewhere (another tab, an external editor) after you opened it, the next autosave notices, reloads the latest version, and tells you your last few seconds of unsaved edits were dropped — instead of quietly overwriting the other copy. Works for both project and global notes.

## ✨ New: Mobile theme toggle

The mobile session-list header gets a light ⇄ dark toggle next to the bell, persisted across reloads. The recent/frequent session tabs also moved from raised pills to underline style — matching the File Browser and DB bubbles, and fixing a dark-mode case where the selected tab was hard to tell apart.

## 🐛 Fix: stale search boxes

The session list, recent-sessions, open-project, and Skills modals stay mounted (rather than unmounting) when closed, which meant they reopened with your previous search term still in the box. They now clear the search on open, and the Skills modal refocuses the input.

name: v1.0.235
body:
## 🐛 Fix: mobile chat no longer renders half off-screen

Opening a chat on the `/m` mobile view could leave the page shifted half off-screen with a dead black right half — browser DevTools device emulation hit it every time. A `scrollIntoView` racing the page-slide animation injected a phantom horizontal scroll into the swipe track, and it never recovered. The track container is now `overflow: clip` — a clipped box cannot be scrolled at all — with a scroll-reset guard as backup. Separately, tablet-width viewports (768–809 px) no longer nest the mobile UI inside the desktop shell's project frame: the mobile redirect now applies only to the top-level window.

## 🐛 Fix: chat stream edge cases

Sending the same prompt repeatedly (say, "继续" three times in a row) could suppress a new turn's user bubble — or cut earlier identical messages from the transcript — because the live stream deduped by prompt text. It now dedups by turn identity and timestamps, so identical prompts are safe.

Jumping to a session from the scheduled-tasks, recent, or pinned panels now refreshes its messages even when the target tab is already active. Previously, messages appended by a scheduled run stayed invisible until you switched away and back.

## 🐛 Fix: notes

Drag-moving a todo (or bullet item) inside a note left an empty checkbox shell behind at the source. The drop now cleans up the emptied item — verified-empty only, so todos you intentionally left blank elsewhere are untouched.

## 🌐 Site: repositioned as an IDE-like workbench

README, npm, and opencockpit.dev now tell the same story: an IDE-like Claude Code workbench you self-host for a whole team. The homepage gained a three-way Compare section (vs the official Claude Code Desktop app, vs Opcode), and a new blog post — "Self-Host a Claude Code GUI for Your Whole Team" (en/zh) — walks through `COCKPIT_HOST`/`--token`, a systemd unit, and the trust model. The GUI-comparison post was refreshed to include the redesigned official Desktop app.
